data.speaker: narrator
data.text: Spirit Meadows. The Keeper rounds a hedge and freezes. A Moglin stands in the open, up on his toes to seem taller than he is, chin high, a too-big grin stretched across his soft face. Behind him, a leash of dark metal. At the end of it, a small spirit.

data.speaker: narrator
data.text: The spirit should glow. It does not. Its color is guttered out toward dull and drab, its light gone sick and faint. It does not look up.

data.speaker: Thistle
data.text: Eyes front, little keeper! You are looking at Thistle, and this meadow is Thistle's now. Mine. Every blade of it.

data.speaker: Thistle
data.text: See my friend here? See how it minds me? That is power, that is. That is what a real keeper looks like. Bet you have not got one of these.

data.speaker: narrator
data.text: The dimmed spirit takes a slow step when the leash pulls. There is no fight left in it. Just the pull, and the dull obeying.

data.speaker: Thistle
data.text: Run along now. Shoo. This is no place for keepers with empty pots.

data.speaker: narrator
data.text: The Keeper cannot help it, not yet, not with empty hands. So the Keeper does the only sense there is. The Keeper runs.
